engineGuide.h2: Mercedes Benz GLE Class Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Mercedes Benz GLE Class across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: Mercedes’ most prolific V6 diesel, fitted to the W166 GLE. Refined and torquey, but notorious for oil seal failures that generate high replacement demand.
commonFailures: Turbocharger oil seal failure leading to smoking exhaust and oil loss (60k-90k miles)
Loose/cracked turbo carrier bolts causing coolant or oil leaks
Oil cooler leaks requiring intake manifold removal
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM642 Mercedes Benz GLE Class engine replacement

entries.title: 2.9L Diesel
entries.history: Mercedes’ advanced inline-six diesel introduced with the W167 GLE. Twin-turbocharged with stepped bowl combustion, delivering exceptional refinement and power.
commonFailures: EGR cooler cracking leading to coolant loss and potential fire risk
Coolant pump-to-vacuum circuit leak (subject to safety recall)
Turbocharger bearing wear due to integrated exhaust manifold design
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM656 Mercedes Benz GLE Class engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Mercedes’ modular four-cylinder diesel launched as the efficient entry point for the W167 GLE. Features stepped bowl combustion and Nissan’s variable geometry turbocharger.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner access difficulties (simplex chain design)
EGR valve and cooler clogging (regular diesel variant)
Coolant pump vacuum circuit leak risk
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM654 Mercedes Benz GLE Class engine replacement

entries.title: 2.1L Diesel
entries.history: The workhorse diesel that powered entry-level W166 GLE models. High-volume unit but suffers from well-documented timing chain vulnerabilities at higher mileages.
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch requiring replacement at 80k-100k miles
Injector failures on 220/250 variants
Simplex chain design prone to premature wear
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM651 Mercedes Benz GLE Class engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol (Biturbo)
entries.history: The 60-degree V6 that replaced the M272, using third-generation direct injection and piezo injectors. Fitted to mid-range W166 petrol GLE models.
commonFailures: Balance shaft gear wear causing timing correlation faults and check engine light
Timing chain guide degradation leading to rattle on startup
Cold start chain rattle requiring camshaft adjuster replacement
entries.cta: Get quotes for M276 DE30 Mercedes Benz GLE Class engine replacement

entries.title: 3.5L Petrol (Naturally Aspirated)
entries.history: The larger-capacity naturally aspirated V6 from the M276 family, fitted to base-spec W166 GLE petrol variants before turbocharging became standard.

entries.title: 3.0L variant
entries.cta: Get quotes for M276 DE35 Mercedes Benz GLE Class engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L MHEV Petrol
entries.history: Mercedes’ modular straight-six introduced with the W167 GLE featuring integrated 48V mild hybrid system (ISG) and electric auxiliary compressor. Represents a technological leap forward.
compatibleVariants: GLE 450, GLE 450 4MATIC, GLE 53 AMG, GLE 53 AMG 4MATIC+
commonFailures: 48V integrated starter-generator (ISG) failures
Electric auxiliary compressor reliability issues
Turbocharger bearing wear
entries.cta: Get quotes for M256 Mercedes Benz GLE Class engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L PHEV Petrol (GLE 400e)
entries.history: Turbocharged four-cylinder petrol engine adapted for plug-in hybrid duty in the W167 GLE 400e, paired with a 31.2kWh battery and electric motor.
commonFailures: Water pump failure (common across M274 family)
High-pressure fuel pump issues
Thermostat failure causing low running temperature and engine light
entries.cta: Get quotes for M274 Mercedes Benz GLE Class engine replacement

entries.title: 5.5L V8 Petrol (W166 GLE 63)
entries.history: The original AMG V8 for the W166 GLE 63, producing dramatic power with twin turbochargers mounted in the V. Discontinued with the W167 generation.
compatibleVariants: GLE 63 AMG, GLE 63 S AMG, GLE 63 AMG Coupe
commonFailures: Cylinder bore scoring
Turbocharger oil feed line issues
High-pressure fuel pump failures
entries.cta: Get quotes for M157 Mercedes Benz GLE Class engine replacement

entries.title: 4.0L V8 Petrol (W167 GLE 63 / 63 S)
entries.history: The hot-vee V8 that powers the W167 GLE 63 and 63 S, with turbochargers mounted inside the V for reduced lag. Produces up to 612bhp in S guise.
compatibleVariants: GLE 63 AMG, GLE 63 S AMG, GLE 63 S AMG 4MATIC+
commonFailures: Cylinder bore wear (reported on higher-mileage units)
Oil consumption issues
Turbocharger wastegate linkage wear
entries.cta: Get quotes for M177 Mercedes Benz GLE Class engine replacement

entries.title: 4.0L V8 Petrol (GLE 580)
entries.history: The non-AMG 4.0-litre V8 fitted to the GLE 580, offering near-AMG power without the full performance tuning. Cylinder deactivation for efficiency.
commonFailures: Cylinder deactivation mechanism failures
Timing chain stretch
Oil separator issues
entries.cta: Get quotes for M176 Mercedes Benz GLE Class engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L PHEV Diesel (GLE 350de)
entries.history: The diesel plug-in hybrid variant of the W167 GLE, combining the OM654 diesel engine with a 100kW electric motor and 31.2kWh battery for up to 68 miles of electric range.
commonFailures: High-voltage battery degradation (affects electric range)
Hybrid control module communication errors
Coolant circulation pump failures for battery thermal management
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM654 PHEV Mercedes Benz GLE Class engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Mercedes Benz GLE Class Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mercedes Benz GLE Class spans two generations - W166 (2015-2019) and W167 (2019-present) - with distinct engine families carrying their own failure patterns. Diesel variants dominate UK demand, particularly the OM642 V6 in the 350d and the OM656 inline-six in the 400d, while petrol models face timing chain vulnerabilities across M276 V6 units . Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Below we break down the most common GLE engine failures, what repair costs look like at dealer vs specialist rates, and when replacement is the smarter financial decision.

problems.group: OM642 V6 Diesel Timing Chain & Turbo Oil Seal Failure
problems.h4: OM642 V6 Diesel Timing Chain & Turbo Oil Seal Failure - Why W166 GLE 350d Engines Fail
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz GLE Class 350d, GLE 350d 4MATIC (2015-2019, OM642 V6 diesel)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: Turbo oil seal 60,000-90,000 miles; Timing chain tensioner wear 80,000-120,000 miles
problems.rootCause: The OM642 V6 diesel suffers from two primary failure modes. The turbocharger oil seal degrades, allowing engine oil to enter the exhaust - producing blue/white smoke and eventually catastrophic turbo failure. Simultaneously, the timing chain tensioner can lose hydraulic pressure, particularly on higher-mileage units, leading to chain rattle and potential jumping of timing if ignored .

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace turbo oil seal, inspect turbo shaft play, clean EGR system
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses smoking issue; only suitable if turbo bearings are intact

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New or reconditioned turbocharger, oil seal, feed lines, gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Resolves oil consumption and power loss; lasts 60k+ miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain, tensioner, guides, camshaft sprockets
repairOptions.longevity: Essential if chain rattle present; prevents catastrophic engine failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit reconditioned OM642 with upgraded internal components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: The only reliable solution if chain has jumped or turbo debris has entered engine

problems.recommendation: For smoking exhaust without loss of power, start with the turbo oil seal replacement at a specialist - the £350-£550 cost is a fraction of a full replacement. However, if you hear timing chain rattle on cold start, do not delay. Chain failure on an OM642 typically writes off the engine, with debris circulating through the oil system. On a GLE 350d worth £12,000-£18,000, a £1,100 specialist timing chain job is excellent preventative maintenance. If the engine has already suffered catastrophic failure, a rebuilt OM642 from a specialist is your most cost-effective route - dealer pricing for a new engine is economically unviable.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt OM642 Mercedes Benz GLE Class engine prices now

problems.group: M276 V6 Petrol Balance Shaft & Timing Chain Guide Failure
problems.h4: M276 V6 Petrol Balance Shaft & Timing Chain Guide Failure - Why W166 GLE 350/400 Engines Prematurely Wear
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz GLE Class 350, 400, 450 (2015-2019, M276 DE35 3.5L & M276 DE30 3.0L biturbo)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: Balance shaft wear 70,000-100,000 miles; Timing chain guide degradation 80,000-110,000 miles
problems.rootCause: The M276 V6 petrol engine has a design vulnerability in its balance shaft assembly - gear wear leads to timing correlation faults and the dreaded check engine light. Additionally, the plastic timing chain guides become brittle with heat cycling and mileage, eventually cracking and allowing the chain to slacken . Mercedes technical service bulletin TSB 07.01.16 specifically addresses these issues across the M276 family.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Timing Chain & Guide Replacement Only
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain, plastic guides, tensioner, camshaft adjuster bolts
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if caught early before debris damages balance shaft

repairOptions.tier: Major - Balance Shaft & Timing System Overhaul
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace balance shaft assembly, timing chain, guides, tensioner, camshaft gears
repairOptions.longevity: Full solution addressing root cause; only recommended if metal debris present

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit reconditioned M276 with upgraded metal timing guides, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if balance shaft failure has circulated debris

problems.recommendation: If you hear rattling on startup from your M276-powered GLE, act immediately. The plastic timing chain guides are the weak link - replacing them at the first sign of noise costs £1,200-£1,800 at a specialist. Ignoring the rattle typically leads to balance shaft gear wear, driving repair costs up to £2,500. On a GLE 400 worth £10,000-£15,000, we recommend specialist overhaul rather than dealer pricing - the savings of £1,000+ are significant, and independent Mercedes specialists are intimately familiar with the M276's failure patterns.
problems.cta: Get quotes for M276 Mercedes Benz GLE Class reconditioned engine

problems.group: M256 & OM656 Inline-Six Turbo Bearing Wear
problems.h4: M256 & OM656 Inline-Six Turbo Bearing Wear - Why W167 GLE 450/53 AMG and GLE 400d Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz GLE Class 450, 53 AMG (2019-present, M256 3.0L MHEV petrol); GLE Class 400d (2019-present, OM656 3.0L twin-turbo diesel)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: Turbo bearing wear 50,000-80,000 miles; EGR cooler cracks 40,000-70,000 miles (OM656)
problems.rootCause: The integrated exhaust manifold design on both M256 petrol and OM656 diesel engines creates extreme thermal stress on turbocharger bearings. Oil coking occurs when the engine is shut off hot, leading to bearing wear and eventual turbo failure . For OM656 diesel variants, the EGR cooler is prone to cracking, causing coolant loss and potential fire risk - subject to multiple safety recalls across 2019-2024 GLE models .

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace EGR cooler unit, coolant flush, gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Critical safety fix; check VIN for recall coverage first

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace turbo cartridge, oil feed lines, gaskets, flush oil system
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses whistling turbo, reduced power; prevents total failure

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt M256 or OM656 Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit reconditioned unit with upgraded turbo cartridges and EGR cooler, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Economically viable only at specialist rates; dealer replacement often exceeds vehicle value

problems.recommendation: For W167 GLE owners, the most urgent issue is the OM656 EGR cooler recall - check your VIN immediately. A cracked cooler can cause sudden coolant loss and engine overheating. For turbo bearing wear, if you hear whistling from the engine bay or experience reduced power, avoid dealer pricing. Specialist turbo cartridge replacement at £1,400-£2,000 is significantly cheaper than a full engine replacement, which should only be considered if debris has circulated through the lubrication system.
problems.cta: Compare OM656 and M256 Mercedes Benz GLE Class replacement engine prices

problems.h4: 48V Mild Hybrid System Failure - Why W167 GLE 450 and 53 AMG Models Won't Start
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz GLE Class 450, 53 AMG (2019-present, M256 with 48V EQ Boost system)
problems.rootCause: The 48V lithium-ion battery pack and integrated starter-generator (ISG) are critical components of Mercedes' EQ Boost mild hybrid system. A loose ground connection - subject to safety recall NHTSA 24V-207 affecting 2019-2024 GLE-Class models - can overheat and create a fire risk . More commonly, the 48V battery fails internally, causing a complete no-start condition that cannot be jump-started. The vehicle becomes "bricked" until the battery is replaced and coded to the car.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Dealer inspection and ground connection repair
repairOptions.longevity: Check your VIN immediately on Mercedes recall portal

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace 48V lithium-ion battery pack, recode to vehicle ECU
repairOptions.longevity: Resolves no-start and electrical system warnings

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace ISG unit, reprogramme control modules
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses charging system failures and hybrid inoperative warnings

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace 48V battery, engine wiring harness, ISG, full coding
repairOptions.longevity: Only required for severe electrical damage or fire risk scenarios

problems.recommendation: If your W167 GLE 450 or 53 AMG fails to start with electrical system warnings, do not assume a conventional jump start will work - the 48V system is entirely separate from the 12V starter battery. First, check your VIN for the ground connection recall. If the 48V battery has failed, specialist pricing of £1,800-£2,500 is significantly lower than dealer rates, and independent Mercedes specialists with proper diagnostic equipment can perform the necessary coding. However, be aware that some dealers claim independent shops cannot recode 48V components - verify your specialist has Mercedes XENTRY diagnostics before proceeding .
problems.cta: Get quotes for M256 48V system repair and engine replacement

problems.group: OM654 & OM651 Four-Cylinder Diesel Timing Chain & EGR Failure
problems.h4: OM654 & OM651 Four-Cylinder Diesel Timing Chain & EGR Failure - Entry-Level GLE Diesel Problems
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz GLE Class 250d (2015-2019, OM651 2.1L); GLE Class 300d (2019-present, OM654 2.0L)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: Timing chain stretch 80,000-120,000 miles; EGR valve clogging 50,000-90,000 miles
problems.rootCause: The OM651 four-cylinder diesel - fitted to the W166 GLE 250d - uses a simplex timing chain design that is prone to stretching over time, particularly on vehicles with extended oil change intervals . The newer OM654 in the W167 GLE 300d suffers from EGR valve and cooler clogging, accelerated by short journeys that prevent proper regeneration cycles. Owners also report intermittent loss of throttle response and engine stalling on OM654-powered GLE 300d models, potentially linked to cam or crank position sensor failure .

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, clean, and decarbonise EGR valve and cooler passages
repairOptions.longevity: Restores response and fuel economy; avoid if heavily coked

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace EGR cooler unit, coolant flush, gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Full solution for persistent clogging or cooler cracks

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain, tensioner, guides, camshaft sprockets
repairOptions.longevity: Essential if chain rattle present; prevents engine jump

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt OM651 or OM654 Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit reconditioned unit with upgraded timing components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if chain has jumped or EGR debris has damaged cylinders

problems.recommendation: For GLE 300d owners experiencing intermittent stalling or loss of throttle response, check engine light diagnostics should prioritise cam and crank position sensors before assuming major engine failure . For OM651 timing chain stretch, listen for a rattling noise on cold start - early replacement at a specialist (£800-£1,200) saves the engine. Once the chain jumps timing, a full replacement is typically the only viable option. Given these vehicles' age and value, we recommend specialist pricing over dealer - the savings of 30-40% are substantial.
problems.cta: Compare OM651 and OM654 Mercedes Benz GLE Class engine prices

problems.group: M176 & M177 V8 Cylinder Bore Wear
problems.h4: M176 & M177 V8 Cylinder Bore Wear - GLE 580 and GLE 63 / 63 S AMG High-Performance Failures
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz GLE Class 580 (2019-present, M176 4.0L V8); GLE Class 63 AMG, 63 S AMG (2019-present, M177 4.0L V8 biturbo)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: Cylinder bore scoring 50,000-80,000 miles; Oil consumption 40,000+ miles
problems.rootCause: The hot-vee design of Mercedes' 4.0-litre V8 places turbochargers within the engine 'V', creating intense heat around the cylinder bores. Over time, this can lead to bore scoring - particularly on higher-mileage performance variants - and increased oil consumption . The M177 in GLE 63 S produces 612bhp, and the extreme cylinder pressures accelerate wear on cylinder linings and piston rings. This is not a preventative maintenance issue - it is a design limitation of high-output engines under sustained load.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Borescope inspection of cylinder walls, compression test, oil analysis
repairOptions.longevity: Essential before committing to any repair

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove engine, machine or re-sleeve cylinders, new pistons and rings, bearings
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses bore scoring; only viable for otherwise sound vehicles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit reconditioned unit with upgraded cylinder liners, performance bearings, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: The only reliable solution for severe bore scoring or oil consumption

problems.recommendation: On a GLE 63 or 63 S worth £35,000-£60,000, cylinder bore scoring is a serious but not terminal issue. Specialist rebuild pricing of £5,000-£8,000 for re-sleeving is significantly more economical than dealer rates. However, if scoring is severe or has damaged multiple cylinders, a full reconditioned engine from a performance specialist (£8,000-£12,000 supply & fit) often provides better long-term value than attempting to rebuild the original unit. Always request a borescope inspection before purchasing any used GLE 63 or 580 - bore scoring is invisible without internal inspection.
problems.cta: Get quotes for M176 and M177 Mercedes Benz GLE Class AMG replacement engines

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mercedes Benz GLE Class
finalCta.paragraph: A failed engine doesn't have to mean the end of your GLE. Whether you own a W166 350d with a smoking OM642 turbo or a W167 53 AMG with 48V system failure, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Mercedes Benz GLE Class specialists. Every rebuilt engine we connect you with includes upgraded components designed to address the original failure point - metal timing guides instead of plastic, reinforced turbo cartridges, and proper EGR cooling solutions. With 12-month warranties, nationwide delivery, and supply & fit available across the UK, you can get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mercedes Benz GLE Class Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mercedes Benz GLE Class and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Mercedes Benz GLE Class Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the GLE Class across W166 and W167 generations is listed below, with compatible engine codes, years and common failure notes.

groups.title: Mercedes Benz GLE Class Diesel Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes Benz GLE Class 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre four-cylinder diesel arrived with the W167 generation in 2019, replacing the older OM651 2.1-litre unit. Using Mercedes’ modular OM654 architecture with stepped bowl combustion and a variable geometry turbo, this engine offers 245hp in GLE 300d guise - remarkably high output for a four-cylinder diesel in this segment. It provides the entry point to the diesel GLE range, balancing respectable economy with sufficient power for a large premium SUV.
commonFailurePoints: camshaft position sensor failure causing intermittent stalling
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz GLE Class 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Mercedes Benz GLE Class 2.1L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.1-litre four-cylinder diesel powered entry-level W166 GLE 250d models from 2015 to 2019. Known internally as OM651, this high-volume Mercedes diesel was carried over from the previous ML-Class, offering 204hp and reasonable economy for a heavy SUV. While refined for its era, the OM651 is now well-documented for timing chain vulnerabilities as mileages climb beyond 80,000 miles.
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz GLE Class 2.1L diesel engines

items.title: Mercedes Benz GLE Class 2.9L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.9-litre inline-six diesel arrived with the W167 GLE in 2018, badged as GLE 350d (272hp) and GLE 400d (330hp). The OM656 engine represents Mercedes’ return to straight-six diesel architecture, featuring stepped bowl combustion, twin sequential turbochargers, and remarkable refinement. It became the backbone of the premium diesel GLE range, offering effortless torque and genuine long-distance comfort. However, early units suffer from well-documented EGR cooler failures that prompted safety recalls.
commonFailurePoints: turbo bearing wear from integrated exhaust manifold heat soak
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz GLE Class 2.9L diesel engines

items.title: Mercedes Benz GLE Class 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 3.0-litre V6 diesel - Mercedes OM642 - powered the W166 GLE 350d from 2015 to 2019, producing 258hp and substantial low-end torque. This engine was the dominant diesel choice for UK GLE buyers during the W166 era, offering a compelling blend of refinement and towing capability. The OM642 is one of Mercedes’ most produced diesel engines, fitted across SUVs, saloons and vans. While robust in many respects, it has two well-known failure points that generate significant engine replacement demand.
commonFailurePoints: Turbocharger oil seal failure causing blue/white smoke
timing chain tensioner wear leading to rattle on startup
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz GLE Class 3.0L diesel engines

groups.title: Mercedes Benz GLE Class Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes Benz GLE Class 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre turbocharged four-cylinder petrol engine appears exclusively in plug-in hybrid form on the W167 GLE 400e. The M274 engine is paired with a 100kW electric motor and a 31.2kWh battery, producing 381hp combined. This PHEV powertrain offers up to 68 miles of electric range, making it a compelling choice for company car drivers seeking low benefit-in-kind tax rates on a large premium SUV.
commonFailurePoints: thermostat failure causing low running temperature
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz GLE Class 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz GLE Class 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.0-litre V6 biturbo petrol - M276 DE30 - powered the W166 GLE 400 from 2015 to 2019, producing 333hp. Positioned above the naturally aspirated 3.5-litre V6, this twin-turbo unit offered significantly more torque and a sportier driving character. It remains a popular choice for buyers seeking petrol refinement without stepping up to AMG models, though timing chain guide degradation is a well-documented weakness as these engines age beyond 80,000 miles.
commonFailurePoints: Balance shaft gear wear causing timing correlation faults
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz GLE Class 3.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz GLE Class 3.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.5-litre naturally aspirated V6 - M276 DE35 - served as the base petrol engine in the W166 GLE 350 from 2015 to 2019, producing 306hp. While less powerful than the 3.0-litre biturbo, this larger-capacity unit offered a simpler, non-turbocharged driving experience with linear power delivery. It was primarily sold in markets where petrol SUVs remained popular, though diesel dominated UK sales. The naturally aspirated design eliminates turbo-related failures but still shares the M276 family’s timing chain vulnerabilities.
commonFailurePoints: valve train noise on cold start
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz GLE Class 3.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz GLE Class 4.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 4.0-litre V8 biturbo appears in two distinct forms on the GLE. The M176 powers the GLE 580 (483hp) as a non-AMG performance option with cylinder deactivation for efficiency. The M177 - a higher-output derivative - powers the GLE 63 (571hp) and GLE 63 S (612hp) AMG models. Both share the hot-vee design with turbochargers mounted inside the engine V for reduced lag, though this creates significant heat management challenges. These engines represent the pinnacle of GLE performance, reserved for the most expensive variants in the range.
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz GLE Class 4.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz GLE Class 5.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 5.5-litre V8 biturbo - M157 - powered the first-generation GLE 63 and GLE 63 S from 2015 to 2019 (W166 generation), producing up to 585hp. This engine was carried over from the preceding ML 63 AMG, offering thunderous performance with twin turbochargers mounted outside the V. While discontinued with the W167 generation in favour of the smaller 4.0-litre M177, the M157 remains a significant engine in GLE history - and one of the most expensive to replace when major failures occur.
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz GLE Class 5.5L petrol engine prices

groups.title: Mercedes Benz GLE Class PHEV Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes Benz GLE Class 2.0L PHEV Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre plug-in hybrid powertrain combines the OM654 diesel engine with a 100kW electric motor and 31.2kWh battery in the GLE 350de (315hp combined). A petrol PHEV variant also exists using the M274 2.0-litre turbo engine in the GLE 400e (381hp combined). Both offer up to 68 miles of electric range and were introduced in 2020 as Mercedes expanded its electrified SUV lineup. These complex powertrains require specialist diagnostic equipment for repair, and replacement costs reflect the integrated hybrid components. Not sure which engine size your Mercedes Benz GLE Class needs? Enter your registration number above - we’ll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Mercedes Benz GLE Class specialists.
commonFailurePoints: coolant circulation pump failures for thermal management
items.cta: Enquire about Mercedes Benz GLE Class 2.0L PHEV engine availability

Enter your registration number above - we’ll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Mercedes Benz GLE Class specialists.

engineYears.h2: Mercedes Benz GLE Class Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: From the 2011 ML-Class launch through the 2015 GLE rebrand to today‘s W167, this section maps every major engine change so you can identify your exact unit and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The W166 M‑Class launched in late 2011 as the third-generation ML, bringing Euro 6 diesel compliance and a new family of V6 petrol engines before the 2015 rebrand to GLE.
keyChanges: All‑new platform with 7G‑Tronic Plus gearbox across all models
First four‑cylinder diesel (OM651) in an ML - 204hp with 500Nm torque
M276 V6 petrol engines replace older M272 units with direct injection
M157 5.5‑litre V8 biturbo powers the ML 63 AMG flagship
mainEngines: M157 - 5.5 litre V8 biturbo petrol (ML 63 AMG)
knownFor: OM651 timing chain stretch is the most common failure on early cars
OM642 suffers oil cooler leaks and swirl flap failures at higher mileages
M276 balance shaft issues began appearing on later V6 units
7G‑Tronic Plus is robust but must be serviced every 60k miles
years.cta: Get quotes for 2011-2015 Mercedes Benz GLE Class replacement engines
years.ticker: OM642 oil cooler leak repair • ML 350 BlueTEC turbo failure • M276 timing chain replacement cost

years.description: The 2015 facelift rebranded the ML to GLE and introduced nine‑speed automatic transmissions for diesel models, plus the first plug-in hybrid in the range.
keyChanges: Rebrand from M‑Class to GLE across all variants
9G‑Tronic replaces 7G‑Tronic Plus on all diesel models
GLE 400 introduced with 3.0‑litre M276 biturbo V6 (333hp)
GLE 500 e plug‑in hybrid launched - 3.0 V6 plus 85kW electric motor
Diesel models gain BlueTEC SCR emissions treatment
mainEngines: M276 DE30 - 3.0 litre V6 biturbo petrol (GLE 400, GLE 450)
knownFor: OM642 remains the UK diesel favourite but turbo oil seal failures are common
M276 DE30 timing chain guide degradation drives petrol replacement demand
9G‑Tronic is smoother and more economical than 7G‑Tronic
GLE 500 e PHEV has limited battery range (30km) and complex hybrid system

keyChanges: Complete platform change to MHA architecture
M256 3.0‑litre inline‑six petrol with 48V EQ Boost mild hybrid
OM656 2.9‑litre inline‑six diesel with twin sequential turbos
OM654 2.0‑litre four‑cylinder diesel for entry GLE 300d
Electric range extended to 68 miles on PHEV variants

M177 - 4.0 litre V8 biturbo (GLE 63 / 63 S AMG)

faq.h2: Mercedes Benz GLE Class Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Mercedes Benz GLE Class engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Rebuilt GLE engine prices range from £2,200 for a 250d OM651 unit to over £10,000 for an AMG V8. Supply-only recon engines typically cost £3,200-£6,800 depending on engine code.
keyPoints: OM651 (250d): £2,200-£2,600 for a reconditioned unit
OM642 (350d): £3,200-£4,500 for V6 diesel recon
OM656 (400d): £5,500-£6,800 due to complex twin-turbo design
M276 V6 petrol: £3,200-£4,000, but verify balance shaft history first
Dealer fitted prices add £1,500-£3,000 in labour vs specialist supply & fit
items.cta: Compare OM642 and OM656 Mercedes Benz GLE Class engine prices now

items.question: What are the most common Mercedes Benz GLE Class engine problems by engine code?
items.answer: OM642 V6 diesel turbos fail from oil seal leaks (£350-£1,600 repair). M276 V6 petrol timing chain guides wear (£1,200-£1,800 specialist fix). OM656 EGR coolers crack (recall affected 2019-2024 models).
keyPoints: OM642: Turbo oil seal failure causes blue/white smoke at 60k-90k miles
M276: Balance shaft and plastic timing chain guide wear from 80k miles
OM656: EGR cooler cracks prompt fire risk recall across 2017-2021 diesel models
OM651: Timing chain stretch on 250d units at 80k-100k miles
M256: 48V starter-generator failures immobilise GLE 450 and 53 AMG
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz GLE Class engine replacement with upgraded components

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in my Mercedes Benz GLE Class?
items.answer: On a GLE worth £12,000-£18,000 with OM642 or OM651 failure, specialist replacement at £3,200-£4,500 is usually worthwhile. On older high-mileage W166 petrol models, weigh repair costs against vehicle value.
keyPoints: GLE 250d/350d (W166) with body mileage under 120k: replacement makes sense
One forum owner faced £26k-£28k dealer quote vs £33k vehicle value - specialist saved 30-40%
M276 petrol replacement at £3,200-£4,000 viable for GLE 400 worth £10k-£15k
Dealer long block + labour = £20k+; specialist recon + fit = £4k-£7k
Get independent shop quotes before committing to dealer pricing
items.cta: Compare specialist vs dealer Mercedes Benz GLE Class engine prices today

items.question: Which Mercedes Benz GLE Class engine is the most reliable?
items.answer: The OM642 V6 diesel in GLE 350d (2015-2019) is widely considered the most reliable GLE engine when properly maintained, despite known turbo oil seal issues. The OM654 in GLE 300d is also solid but watch for EGR clogging.
keyPoints: OM642: Robust V6 with generous torque - best balance of reliability and running costs
OM651 four-cylinder diesel: Decent but timing chain needs monitoring after 180,000 km
M276 petrol V6: Very smooth and free from diesel DPF/EGR issues, but timing chain risk exists
M157 AMG V8: Reliable if maintained but very high running costs
Best used buy: GLE 350 CDI (OM642) - durable, enjoyable and reliable
items.cta: Find a reconditioned Mercedes Benz GLE Class OM642 engine with warranty

items.question: What causes OM642 turbo failure on my Mercedes Benz GLE 350d?
items.answer: The turbo oil seal degrades from heat cycling, allowing engine oil to enter the exhaust. This produces blue/white smoke and can lead to complete turbo failure. Boost pressure actuator issues also cause intermittent limp mode.
keyPoints: Failed turbo oil seal is the primary cause of smoking exhaust on OM642
Boost pressure regulator (actuator) can cause jerking under heavy acceleration
Intermittent codes like 2510 appear without check engine light
Actual boost values fluctuating between 34-90% indicates actuator wear
Replacement cost: £350-£550 for seal-only, £1,100-£1,600 for full turbo
items.cta: Get quotes for OM642 turbo repair or Mercedes Benz GLE Class replacement engine

items.question: How long does a Mercedes Benz GLE Class engine last?
items.answer: Well-maintained OM642 and OM656 diesel GLE engines regularly exceed 150,000-200,000 miles. Petrol M276 V6 units typically reach 120,000-150,000 miles with proper timing chain maintenance. M256 mild hybrid longevity less proven.
keyPoints: OM642 V6 diesel: 150k-200k+ miles with regular servicing
OM651 four-cylinder: 120k-150k miles before timing chain attention needed
M276 V6 petrol: 120k-150k miles but timing chain guides are the weak point
Service interval is 12 months or 15,500 miles - do not extend
High-mileage OM642 examples running strong at 20,000+ miles per year
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz GLE Class replacement engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: Should I buy a petrol or diesel Mercedes Benz GLE Class?
items.answer: Diesel (OM642/OM656) is better for high-mileage motorway driving with 30-35mpg achievable. Petrol (M276) suits lower-mileage urban use but returns just 8-9 km/l (approx 22-25mpg). Diesel replacement demand is significantly higher in the UK.
keyPoints: Diesel GLE 350d/400d: Superior torque and towing capability, 30-35 mpg motorway
Petrol GLE 350/400: Smoother, quieter, no DPF/EGR diesel issues but fuel consumption high
Urban petrol drivers report approximately 8.9 km/l fuel economy
Petrol engines simpler to maintain but fuel costs add up for high-mileage users
GLE 350 CDI recommended as the best all-rounder for reliability and economy
items.cta: Get tailored Mercedes Benz GLE Class engine advice for your driving needs

items.question: What is the 48V mild hybrid system on Mercedes Benz GLE 450 and 53 AMG?
items.answer: The M256 engine uses a 48V integrated starter-generator (ISG) and lithium-ion battery for electric boost and coasting. When the 48V battery fails, the vehicle won't start and requires specialist replacement costing £1,800-£2,500.
keyPoints: 48V system provides EQ Boost: extra 22hp and torque assistance
Also powers the electric auxiliary compressor for lag-free turbo response
Failed 48V battery leaves the car completely immobilised - no jump start possible
Replacement requires Mercedes XENTRIS diagnostics and coding
Dealer pricing £2,200-£3,500; specialists typically £1,800-£2,500
items.cta: Enquire about Mercedes Benz GLE Class M256 48V system repair costs

items.question: Does Mercedes Benz GLE Class timing chain replacement need doing? How much does it cost?
items.answer: Yes, the M276 petrol V6 has plastic timing chain guides that degrade from 80,000 miles. Replacement cost is £1,200-£1,800 at a specialist versus £1,800-£2,600 at a dealer. Cold start rattle is the classic warning sign.
keyPoints: M276 V6 uses a timing chain, not a belt - but plastic guides fail
Genuine Mercedes timing chain part number 276-050-24-16 for M276 engines
Cold start rattle indicates tensioner or guide wear
Ignoring rattle leads to timing jump and piston-valve contact
Preventative replacement saves £2,000+ vs full engine replacement
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz GLE Class M276 timing chain replacement quotes

items.question: Can I fit a GLE 63 AMG engine into my standard Mercedes Benz GLE?
items.answer: Theoretically possible but prohibitively expensive. The M177 V8 requires different engine mounts, gearbox, cooling system, wiring harness, ECU, and transmission. Most specialists advise selling your GLE and buying an AMG instead.
keyPoints: Different engine mounts, subframe, and transmission required
Entire wiring harness and all ECUs must be swapped
Cooling system is completely different on V8 models
Brakes, suspension, and exhaust all need upgrading
Labour alone often exceeds £10,000 plus parts
items.cta: Get quotes for standard Mercedes Benz GLE Class engine replacement instead
